name: Uncertainty-Based Bayesian Arbitration
description: Probabilistic dual-system reinforcement learning agent where both the model-free (habitual) and model-based (goal-directed) systems maintain Gaussian posterior beliefs over action values. The key differentiator from Formulation 1 is that arbitration is driven by relative uncertainty (inverse variance / precision) rather than a fixed training-count decay. When the model-free system has higher uncertainty than the model-based system, goal-directed control dominates (omega → 1); when model-free uncertainty is lower (well-practiced actions), habitual control dominates (omega → 0). This produces adaptive arbitration: the agent can re-engage goal-directed control even in familiar environments if conditions change and model-free predictions become unreliable. The model-free system uses Kalman-filter-style Bayesian updates; the model-based system computes value means and variances from the learned transition model. Based on Rangel, Camerer & Montague (2008) and Rangel (2013), postulates P1–P5.

variables.name: state
variables.description: Agent's current grid cell encoded as (x, y) tuple

variables.name: model_free_value_mean
variables.description: Posterior mean of the habitual action value for each (state, action) pair. Updated via Kalman-filter-style Bayesian TD updates. Represents the agent's best estimate of cached action value.

variables.name: model_free_value_variance
variables.description: Posterior variance (uncertainty) of the habitual action value for each (state, action) pair. Starts at sigma2_0 (high uncertainty) and shrinks with experience via Kalman updates. Drives arbitration: lower variance = higher MF reliability.

variables.name: model_based_value_mean
variables.description: Posterior mean of the goal-directed action value, computed prospectively each step from the learned transition model and current hunger-modulated desirability. Recomputed every decision step.

variables.name: model_based_value_variance
variables.description: Uncertainty of the goal-directed action value, computed from outcome variance in the transition model plus observation noise scaled by experience count. Decreases as the transition model becomes more certain.

variables.name: arbitration_weight
variables.description: Reliability-weighted proportion of goal-directed control. Computed per (state, action) via sigmoid of the difference in uncertainties: omega = sigmoid(kappa * (sigma2_MF - sigma2_MB)). When MF uncertainty > MB uncertainty, omega → 1 (favor GD). Adaptive and reversible.

variables.name: fused_value_estimate
variables.description: Precision-weighted mean of model-based and model-free value estimates. Used as the final action value for softmax selection.

variables.name: hunger_drive
variables.description: Internal physiological state representing current need. Modulates goal-directed outcome desirability. Rises each step, drops upon consuming food.

variables.name: decision_time_desirability
variables.description: Current subjective value of each possible outcome, modulated by hunger. Food: r_D = h * r_food. Non-food: r_D = c_step.

variables.name: learned_transition_model
variables.description: Agent's estimate of action-outcome contingency p_hat(o|a,s). Updated incrementally from observations.

variables.name: model_based_experience_count
variables.description: Number of times each (state, action) pair has been experienced, used to scale down model-based observation noise. Higher count = lower MB uncertainty.

parameters.name: prior_variance
parameters.source: Uninformative prior; standard Bayesian initialization for both systems

parameters.name: prior_mean
parameters.source: Neutral prior — no initial preference for any action

parameters.name: mf_observation_noise_variance
parameters.source: Reflects noisy experienced-reward signals in model-free learning (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)

parameters.name: mb_observation_noise_variance
parameters.source: Lower noise — model-based uses structured knowledge and is inherently less noisy (Rangel, 2013)

parameters.name: transition_model_learning_rate
parameters.source: Model-based systems update faster (Rangel, 2013; postulate P2)

parameters.name: arbitration_sigmoid_sensitivity
parameters.source: Controls steepness of sigmoid arbitration; tuned for gradual transitions between control modes

parameters.name: hunger_rise_rate
parameters.source: Per-step hunger increase when not eating

parameters.name: base_food_reward
parameters.source: Normalized reward unit matching env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0

rules.description: Hunger dynamics: hunger rises each step by eta and drops by phi when food is consumed. Clipped to [0, 1].
rules.source_postulate: Assumption: state-dependent desirability; internal hunger modulates goal-directed valuation

rules.description: Decision-time desirability: subjective value of food outcome is hunger-modulated; non-food outcome incurs step cost.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — goal-directed value uses current desirability r^D(o|s)

rules.description: Model-based value mean: computed prospectively from learned transition model and current desirability. For eat at food: weighted sum of outcome desirabilities. For movement: step cost plus discounted bootstrap from MF at next state.
Model-based value variance: sum of outcome variance from the transition model plus observation noise scaled down by experience count. Captures both outcome stochasticity and estimation uncertainty.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — model-based system maintains structured knowledge enabling uncertainty quantification

rules.description: Model-free Kalman gain: ratio of prior variance to total variance (prior + observation noise). Controls how much the new observation shifts the posterior mean.
rules.source_postulate: P3 — habitual system updates via incremental experience; Bayesian formulation of TD learning

rules.description: Model-free posterior mean update: Kalman-filtered TD update. The 'observation' is the TD target (r_received + gamma * max future MF mean).
rules.pseudocode:
td_target = r_received + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)
mu_MF[(s,a)] = mu_MF.get((s,a), 0.0) + K * (td_target - mu_MF.get((s,a), 0.0))
rules.source_postulate: P3 — V_H(a|s) updated via incremental experience (TD), not decomposed into contingency and desirability

rules.description: Model-free posterior variance update: variance shrinks by factor (1-K) after each observation. Captures increasing confidence with experience.
rules.source_postulate: P3 — habitual system's reliability increases with consistent experience

rules.description: Transition model update: after observing outcome o_obs from action a in state s, update p_hat toward the observed outcome.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — action-outcome contingency p(o|a,s) is learned and updated from experience

rules.description: Uncertainty-based arbitration weight via sigmoid: when MF variance exceeds MB variance, sigmoid output > 0.5, favoring goal-directed. When MF variance is lower, sigmoid < 0.5, favoring habitual. Kappa controls sensitivity.
rules.source_postulate: P1, P4 — two controllers compete; arbitration based on reliability (uncertainty) with adaptive, reversible dynamics

rules.description: Fused action value: precision-weighted combination of model-based and model-free value means, weighted by the arbitration parameter omega.
rules.source_postulate: P1 — two dissociable valuation systems cooperate via weighted mixing for action selection

rules.description: Softmax action selection over fused values.
rules.pseudocode:
probs = softmax([beta * mu_hat[s][a] for a in actions])
selected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=probs)
rules.source_postulate: Assumption: common currency of value enters common action-selection mechanism

rules.description: Increment model-based experience count for the executed (state, action) pair.
rules.source_postulate: Tracking experience for MB variance scaling

decision_logic.description: Each step: (1) Read state s=(x,y), food resources, hunger h. (2) Compute hunger-modulated desirability r_D (R2). (3) For each action, compute model-based mean mu_MB and variance sigma2_MB from transition model (R3, R4). (4) Retrieve model-free posterior means mu_MF and variances sigma2_MF. (5) Compute per-action arbitration weight omega via sigmoid of variance difference (R7). (6) Fuse values into mu_hat (R8). (7) Select action via softmax (R9). (8) After execution: update hunger (R1), transition model (R6), MF posteriors via Kalman update (R5a-R5c), increment N_MB (R10).
pseudocode: food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in perception.resources.get('food', [])]
# R3-R4: Compute model-based mean and variance for each action
mu_MB[s][a] = c_step + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)
mu_hat_a = omega_a * mu_MB[s][a] + (1 - omega_a) * mu_MF.get((s, a), 0.0)
probs = softmax([beta * mu_hat_a for a in actions])
r_received = r_food if ate_food else c_step
p_hat[(s, action, o_obs)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5) + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5))
for o in outcomes where o != o_obs:
p_hat[(s, action, o)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5) + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5))
# R5a-R5c: Kalman-filter update for model-free posterior
K = sigma2_MF.get((s, action), sigma2_0) / (sigma2_MF.get((s, action), sigma2_0) + sigma2_obs_MF)
td_target = r_received + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)
mu_MF[(s, action)] = mu_MF.get((s, action), 0.0) + K * (td_target - mu_MF.get((s, action), 0.0))
sigma2_MF[(s, action)] = (1 - K) * sigma2_MF.get((s, action), sigma2_0)

perception_to_variables.food_positions: perception.resources.food — list of {x, y, type, palatability} dicts
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_position: any(f['x'] == perception.x and f['y'] == perception.y for f in perception.resources.get('food', []))
perception_to_variables.ate_food: action was 'eat' and perception.last_action_result indicates consumption

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action at food location → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions → internal step cost c_step = -0.01

expected_behaviors.description: Adaptive goal-directed dominance in novel states: in never-visited states, sigma2_MF is at prior (1.0) and sigma2_MB is also at prior (1.0), so omega ≈ 0.5. As the agent gains MB experience (transition model improves), sigma2_MB drops faster than sigma2_MF initially, pushing omega > 0.5 toward goal-directed control.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run agent for 20 steps in new area → assert sigma2_MB decreases faster than sigma2_MF for eat-at-food actions → assert omega > 0.5 for those actions

expected_behaviors.description: Uncertainty-driven habit transition: after extensive experience, sigma2_MF shrinks to near zero via Kalman updates. When sigma2_MF < sigma2_MB, omega < 0.5 and habitual values dominate.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run 300 steps in stable environment → assert sigma2_MF < sigma2_MB for frequently visited (s,a) → assert omega < 0.3

expected_behaviors.description: Reversible arbitration: if the environment changes (food moves), MB predictions update quickly but MF values become stale (high effective error), causing sigma2_MF to effectively increase relative to sigma2_MB, re-engaging goal-directed control.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Train 200 steps → relocate food → assert omega increases for affected states as MF predictions become unreliable

expected_behaviors.description: Devaluation sensitivity in goal-directed mode: when omega > 0.5, reducing hunger immediately lowers mu_MB for eat (since r_D_food = h * r_food), reducing the fused value and suppressing eating.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.05 with high omega → assert mu_hat[eat] is low → assert agent avoids eating

expected_behaviors.description: Devaluation insensitivity in habitual mode: when omega < 0.5, mu_MF (which learned from past high-hunger eating) dominates, so reducing hunger does NOT immediately suppress eating.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.05 with low omega (overtrained) → assert mu_hat[eat] ≈ mu_MF[eat] (still high from past learning) → agent may still eat

references: Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9, 545–556.
Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724.
